reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_73]]
Speaker 2: When Covid first hit the United States, many universities rushed to roll out online degree programs.

[[BLOCK:srt_74]]
Speaker 2: But we already had well-established online programs in place by then.

[[BLOCK:srt_75]]
Speaker 2: So the real challenge wasn’t starting from scratch—it was figuring out how to make our existing programs even better and continuously refine and upgrade them with the latest technology.

[[BLOCK:srt_76]]
Speaker 3: So how does Golden Gate University’s online education support students in balancing their studies with career development?

[[BLOCK:srt_77]]
Speaker 3: And sometimes the dean intentionally holds his gaze on the camera a bit longer—after all, he’s not usually looking directly at you, right?

[[BLOCK:srt_78]]
Speaker 3: Yes, his eyes drift slightly from time to time. Have you noticed?

[[BLOCK:srt_79]]
Speaker 3: Right, right—he’s really trying to keep his eyes on the camera, isn’t he?

[[BLOCK:srt_80]]
Speaker 3: Exactly—otherwise, when he watches the recording later—

[[BLOCK:srt_81]]
Speaker 3: All right, please continue—

[[BLOCK:srt_82]]
Speaker 2: Right, I just—

[[BLOCK:srt_83]]
Speaker 2: I was looking at the wrong camera just now.

[[BLOCK:srt_84]]
Speaker 1: No worries—just, yes, look over there.
